body:
## Summary
Adds the foundational user-authentication module for the note-taking app (issue #1). Self-contained, zero-dependency (Node built-in `crypto` only) and in-memory, matching the project scaffold.

- `src/auth.js` exposes `register`, `login`, and `verifyToken` (plus an `AuthError` and a `_reset` test helper).
- `verifyToken(token)` is the seam the upcoming notes-CRUD (#2) and sync (#3) services will use to resolve a credential to the current user.
- Passwords are hashed with `scrypt` + a per-user random salt at a fixed 64-byte key length; plaintext is never stored. Login/verify failures throw a single uniform `Invalid credentials` error, and an always-run hash on login avoids leaking user existence via timing. (Refinements folded in from the review on #1.)

Deliberately avoids JWT/bcrypt and any HTTP framework to keep the greenfield first cut dependency-free; the functional API satisfies every acceptance criterion. Wiring to routes and a persistent store are deferred to later issues.

Closes #1.

## Test plan
`npm test` (`node --test`) — green. 9 new automated cases in `test/auth.test.js` plus the pre-existing test, 10 passing total:

- register success + duplicate-username rejection + missing-field rejection
- login success returns token + user
- login failure on wrong password and on unknown user
- verifyToken resolves a token to the correct user; bogus/missing token rejected
- password-stored-hashed: wrong password fails while the correct one succeeds, and the public user object carries no credential material

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
title: feat(auth): add user registration, login, and token verification
